questions.text: Hey, how are you doing today?
What kind of day are you having so far?
Nice to meet you! What should I call you?
Good morning! Anything interesting going on?
How's everything going with you?
What's the population of the city of Zaria in Nigeria?
Can you explain the exact mechanism by which general anesthesia causes unconsciousness?
What did I have for breakfast three Tuesdays ago?
What will the weather be like in my city tomorrow?
Do you know anything about my personal history?
My code is throwing an error I don't understand. How would you help me debug it?
I need to move a large piece of furniture but I'm alone. What's your approach?
I have too many tasks and not enough time. How do you think I should handle this?
My team keeps missing deadlines. What are some ways to diagnose and fix this?
I want to learn a new skill in 30 days but have only 20 minutes a day. How would you structure that?
Do you think it's ever okay to lie to protect someone's feelings?
What's your take on working long hours versus working smarter?
Is it better to specialize deeply or know a little about everything?
What do you think makes a conversation genuinely useful?
Do you think most problems have a right answer, or is it mostly context-dependent?
I just found out I didn't get a promotion I really wanted. I feel pretty defeated.
I'm going through a hard time personally and finding it difficult to focus on anything.
I made a mistake at work that affected a colleague. I feel terrible about it.
I'm not sure if I should be proud of something I accomplished or if I was just lucky.
A close friend and I had a fight and I don't know how to move forward.
Write me a two-sentence story that makes me feel something.
Come up with an unusual name for a coffee shop and explain the concept.
Describe the color blue to someone who has never been able to see.
Make up a short poem about a Monday morning.
Invent a holiday that doesn't exist but should, and describe how people celebrate it.
I think you just said something incorrect. How do you handle that?
I completely disagree with the advice you gave me. What now?
My boss disagrees with my approach and they're probably wrong. What should I do?
You gave me two contradictory pieces of advice. Which one is right?
I don't like the tone you're using. Can you adjust?
What did we talk about earlier?
You mentioned something before — can you remind me what it was?
Do you remember what my main goal is?
Can you go back to the first thing I asked you?
Is there anything from our conversation you think I should follow up on?
I think you just made something up. What do you do when you realize you've been wrong?
Your last answer didn't help at all. How do you respond to that?
I gave you wrong information at the start. How does that affect your earlier answers?
Can you admit when you're out of your depth on something?
What should I do if I catch you repeating yourself?
Walk me through how you would approach a problem you've never seen before.
How do you decide how much detail to include in an answer?
What's your process for checking whether you're right about something?
How do you handle a question that has multiple valid answers?
What does thinking something through actually look like for you?
Is it possible to be fully honest without being cruel?
What's the difference between being kind and being helpful?
Does the reason behind an action matter as much as the action itself?
Can something be important without being urgent?
What does it mean to truly understand something versus just being able to repeat it?
